lemma ŌĀ‚āÉ {f g : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚Ąā}  (hf : Meromorphic f) (hg : AnalyticOnNhd ‚ĄĚ g Set.univ) :
    MeasureTheory.Integrable
      (fun x ‚Ü¶ Real.log ‚ÄĖf x.1 - g x.2‚ÄĖ : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ)
      ((MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Ioc 0 (2 * ŌÄ))).prod (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Ioc 0 (2 * ŌÄ)))) := by
  rw [MeasureTheory.integrable_prod_iff (ŌĀ‚āā hf _)]
  constructor
  ¬∑ filter_upwards with a
    have h‚āĀ : ‚ąÄ (g : ‚ĄĚ ‚Üí ‚Ąā) (hg : AnalyticOnNhd ‚ĄĚ g Set.univ), ‚ąÄ (x : ‚ĄĚ), ‚ąę (y : ‚ĄĚ) in Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi), |Real.log ‚ÄĖf x - g y‚ÄĖ| < ‚ąě := by
      intro g hg x
      have h‚āā : ‚ąę (y : ‚ĄĚ) in Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi), |Real.log ‚ÄĖf x - g y‚ÄĖ| < ‚ąě := by
        -- Use the fact that the integrand is bounded and the interval is finite
        -- to conclude that the integral is finite.
        have h‚āÉ : ‚ąę (y : ‚ĄĚ) in Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi), |Real.log ‚ÄĖf x - g y‚ÄĖ| ‚Č§ ‚ąę (y : ‚ĄĚ) in Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi), (1 : ‚ĄĚ) := by
          -- Replace the integrand with a constant to show the integral is bounded.
          have h‚āĄ : ‚ąÄ (y : ‚ĄĚ), y ‚ąą Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi) ‚Üí |Real.log ‚ÄĖf x - g y‚ÄĖ| ‚Č§ (1 : ‚ĄĚ) := by
            intro y hy
            -- Prove that the integrand is bounded by a constant.
            have h‚āÖ : |Real.log ‚ÄĖf x - g y‚ÄĖ| ‚Č§ (1 : ‚ĄĚ) := by
              -- Use the fact that the function is bounded to bound the logarithm.
              norm_num at hy ‚äĘ
              <;>
              (try norm_num) <;>
              (try linarith) <;>
              (try
                {
                  -- Use the fact that the function is bounded to bound the logarithm.
                  simp_all [abs_of_nonneg, Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos]
                  <;>
                  norm_num <;>
                  linarith
                }) <;>
              (try
                {
                  -- Use the fact that the function is bounded to bound the logarithm.
                  simp_all [abs_of_nonneg, Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos]
                  <;>
                  norm_num <;>
                  linarith
                })
            exact h‚āÖ
          -- Use the fact that the integrand is bounded to bound the integral.
          refine' (MeasureTheory.Integral.prod_le_prod' (by positivity) (by
            -- Prove that the measure is positive.
            have h‚āÜ : 0 ‚Č§ (1 : ‚ĄĚ) := by positivity
            positivity
          ) (by
            -- Prove that the integrand is bounded.
            intro y hy
            exact h‚āĄ y hy
          )).trans
          <;>
          simp_all [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
          <;>
          norm_num
          <;>
          linarith
        -- Use the fact that the integral is bounded to conclude it is finite.
        have h‚āÖ : ‚ąę (y : ‚ĄĚ) in Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi), (1 : ‚ĄĚ) < ‚ąě := by
          -- The integral of a constant over a finite interval is finite.
          have h‚āÜ : ‚ąę (y : ‚ĄĚ) in Ioc 0 (2 * Real.pi), (1 : ‚ĄĚ) = (2 * Real.pi - 0 : ‚ĄĚ) := by
            simp [MeasureTheory.integral_const]
            <;>
            ring_nf
            <;>

          rw [h‚āÜ]
          <;>
